nodes.title: Strait of Hormuz
nodes.body:
~20-21 Mb/d of crude + products (EIA 2023) — roughly 1/5 of global oil — plus ~1/5 of global LNG (Qatar, UAE).

No full bypass. Saudi East-West pipeline ~5 Mb/d and UAE Fujairah line ~1.5 Mb/d cover only a fraction. Physics: the oil is on one side, the buyers on the other.

Iranian shore batteries / IRGC navy vs US 5th Fleet (Bahrain). Insurance markets price the risk daily.

No replacement possible. Mitigation = pipelines (years) + SPR draws (months).

Closure: oil +$30-60/bbl in days, tanker war-risk premia 10-50x, Asia refiners (CHN/IND/JPN/KOR take ~80% of flow) hit first, then global goods inflation.

The single highest-leverage physical point on Earth for the price of energy. Insurance pricing reacts faster than news.

nodes.title: Malacca / Singapore Strait
nodes.body:
~23.7 Mb/d oil (EIA 2023) + the dominant share of China-bound container and bulk traffic. Singapore = world #1 bunkering hub.

Shortest sea path Indian Ocean → East Asia. Alternatives (Sunda, Lombok) add 3-5 days; the Kra canal has been studied for a century and never built.

Littoral states (SG/MY/ID) in peacetime; US Navy in conflict. This is what Chinese planners call the 'Malacca dilemma'.

None. China's mitigation = overland pipelines (Myanmar, Central Asia) + NSR + strategic reserves — partial at best.

Interdiction = energy blockade of Northeast Asia. China's pipeline + reserve buffer ≈ months, not years.

Every China-conflict scenario prices through this strait. Watch insurance and charter rates as the sensor.

nodes.title: Suez Canal / Red Sea
nodes.body:
~12-15% of global trade pre-2023. After Houthi attacks began Nov 2023, transits fell ~50-65%; Cape reroute adds 10-14 days.

Only sea shortcut Europe↔Asia. The 2023-26 crisis is a live experiment: trade reroutes rather than stops — cost, not collapse.

Egypt (canal revenue), Houthis (cheap drones/ASBMs vs $2M interceptors — cost-exchange inversion), US/EU naval escorts.

Cape route = instant but +30-40% distance. Effects: tonne-mile demand up, effective fleet capacity down.

Freight spikes (Drewry WCI tripled in early 2024), EU energy/goods inflation, Egypt fiscal stress (canal FX revenue).

Proof that a $20k drone can tax $1T of trade. Asymmetric-cost weapons are repricing every chokepoint.

nodes.title: Panama Canal
nodes.body:
~5% of seaborne trade; critical for US East Coast ↔ Asia and US LNG/LPG exports.

Fresh-water constrained: each transit uses ~50M gallons from Gatún Lake. 2023-24 drought cut slots from 36 to ~22-24/day and draft limits.

Panama Canal Authority; US political pressure resurfaced 2025 (ports, fees).

Water projects = years. Climate variance is now a recurring throughput tax.

LNG/container reroutes via Suez or Cape; queue auctions hit $4M/slot at peak.

First major chokepoint where the binding constraint is rainfall. Climate → logistics transmission is real and measurable.

nodes.title: Taiwan Strait
nodes.body:
~48% of the global container fleet transited in 2022 (Bloomberg). Plus: the island at its edge makes ~90% of leading-edge logic.

Geography stacks two monopolies: a trade artery and the semiconductor apex on the same 100 miles.

PLA exercises vs US 7th Fleet + Japan. 'Blockade without invasion' is the studied scenario.

Trade reroutes east of Taiwan (days). Chip capacity: 4-5 years per fab, and the people don't move with the buildings.

Blockade = simultaneous shipping shock + chip embargo on the world. Insurance withdrawal alone could halt traffic before a shot is fired.

The most consequential square on the board. Tail risk, not base case — but everything chips-related is a derivative of this node.

Russia (Rosatom runs the route authority). China calls it the 'Polar Silk Road'.

Ice-class fleets = years; insurance and SAR coverage remain thin.

Long-run: erodes Suez/Malacca rents; near-run: a sanctions-evasion corridor.

nodes.title: ASML — Veldhoven
nodes.body:
The only maker of EUV lithography machines on Earth. €28.3B revenue 2024. High-NA tools ~€350M each.

30 years and ~$10B+ of accumulated R&D, plus a 5,000-supplier tree (Zeiss optics, Trumpf lasers, Cymer source). No second source exists or is close.

Dutch state under US pressure: EUV never exported to China; DUV restrictions tightened 2023-25.

A competitor from scratch: 10-15+ years. China's SMEE is generations behind on EUV.

ASML stops shipping → leading-edge capacity growth stops globally within ~2 years. Single-company kill switch for Moore's law.

The deepest moat in the industrial world. Its order book is a 2-3 year leading indicator of global fab build.

nodes.title: Zeiss SMO — Oberkochen
nodes.body:
Sole supplier of EUV optics — mirrors polished to sub-nanometer figure error ('if a mirror were Germany, the largest bump would be 1mm').

One firm, essentially one site, feeding the one EUV maker. The supply chain narrows to a town in Swabia.

Germany; integrated with ASML via long-term exclusivity.

Optics capability = decades of accumulated process knowledge. Harder to copy than the lithography integration itself.

Site loss (fire/quake/sabotage) = EUV production halt measured in years. A genuine single-point-of-failure for civilization-scale tech.

Most analyses stop at ASML. The real apex is one layer deeper. Concentration risk is fractal.

nodes.body:
~90% of ≤5nm leading-edge logic, ~2/3 of global foundry revenue. Every NVIDIA AI GPU made here. Publishes monthly revenue.

Process knowledge compounds: yield learning curves mean the leader gets cheaper while followers burn cash. Customers (Apple, NVIDIA) co-fund the lead.

Taiwan. Which is the point — the 'silicon shield' doctrine is national strategy.

Loss = global electronics/AI freeze. US Arizona fabs cover a single-digit % of output, without advanced packaging.

TSMC monthly revenue is the cleanest free macro-AI dataset that exists. Set a recurring check.

Sanctioned since 2019, survived, and became the integration point of the whole domestic stack (chip design, networking, HarmonyOS).

Beijing's priority firm; access to SMIC capacity is politically allocated.

Ascend competitiveness gated by SMIC yields + domestic HBM (CXMT) — packaging is the choke.

Credible Ascend scale = bifurcated global AI hardware market, permanently.

nodes.title: Bayan Obo — Inner Mongolia
nodes.body:
Largest rare-earth deposit on Earth. China overall: ~85-90% of REE refining, ~90% of sintered NdFeB magnets.

The choke isn't ore (REEs aren't rare) — it's separation chemistry + environmental tolerance + 30 years of scale. Mining is easy; refining is the moat.

Beijing. April 2025 licensing regime put dual-use REE exports under case-by-case control — the lever is now institutionalized.

Western separation capacity: 3-5 yrs per plant; full magnet chain: longer.

Embargo hits EV motors, wind turbines, and precision-guided munitions in the same month.

China's most-rehearsed economic weapon (2010 Japan, 2025 regime). The question is calibration, not capability.

nodes.title: Morowali — Indonesia nickel
nodes.body:
>50% of global nickel now Indonesian, built in under a decade via the 2020 raw-ore export ban forcing onshore smelting (RKEF/HPAL), with Chinese capital.

THE template of modern resource nationalism: ban the ore, capture the value-add. It worked — and crashed the nickel price, breaking LME trading in Mar 2022 en route.

Jakarta sets policy; Chinese firms (Tsingshan et al.) own much of the capacity.

Competing supply: years; most Western nickel projects now uneconomic at Indonesian cost.

Indonesia is now trying the same playbook on bauxite, copper, tin. Every resource state watched and learned.

Study this node to predict the next ten: ore-ban nationalism is now the default move for any mineral-rich middle power.

nodes.title: Greenbushes — lithium
nodes.body:
World's largest hard-rock lithium mine. Lithium price round-trip: >$80k/t (2022) → <$11k/t (2024-25) — an ~85% crash.

Demonstrates the commodity trap: 3-yr supply response to a price spike overshoots, because mines are lumpy and everyone FIDs at the top.

Albemarle/Tianqi/IGO JV — itself a US-China entanglement.

Hard-rock mine: 3-5 yrs; brine: longer. Supply is now being curtailed at the bottom — the cycle's other half.

Sustained low price kills Western marginal projects → Chinese-cost supply gains share → next shortage pre-loaded for late 2020s.

Commodity alpha = betting against the crowd's extrapolation at both extremes. Lithium just taught the full cycle in 4 years.

Russian retaliation on enrichment = fuel squeeze on US reactors mid-restart-wave.

nodes.title: Permian Basin
nodes.body:
~6.3-6.6 Mb/d; carried US output to ~13.3-13.5 Mb/d (world #1). Shale = the price-elastic swing supply that broke OPEC's pricing power 2014-20.

Decline treadmill: wells fall ~60-70% in year one, so output = f(continuous drilling). Capital discipline era (post-2020) means growth stalls near $70-80 WTI.

Dozens of public/private E&Ps + Wall Street's tolerance for capex.

Response time: 6-12 months — uniquely fast for oil. But inventory of tier-1 acreage is finite; 'US production has peaked' (Diamondback letter, 2025) is now a live thesis [md confidence].

Permian plateau = pricing power migrates back to OPEC+ in the late 2020s.

The single most important question in oil this decade: is the Permian plateauing? Evidence: mixed, leaning yes.

nodes.lead: Response time: 6-12 months — uniquely fast for oil. But inventory of tier-1 acreage is finite; 'US production has peaked' (Diamondback letter, 2025) is now a live thesis [md confidence].
nodes.consequence: Permian plateau = pricing power migrates back to OPEC+ in the late 2020s.
nodes.signal_desc: Rig counts vs output (efficiency), tier-1 inventory estimates, gas-oil ratio creep (aging signal).
nodes.analyst_note: The single most important question in oil this decade: is the Permian plateauing? Evidence: mixed, leaning yes.

nodes.title: Ras Laffan — Qatar LNG
nodes.body:
QatarEnergy expanding 77 → 142 mtpa by ~2030 — the largest LNG buildout in history, at the world's lowest cost (~$2-4/MMBtu breakeven).

North Field is geology's gift: one reservoir, conventional, shallow. Qatar can win a price war it chooses to start.

Doha. Exports transit Hormuz — chokepoint-coupled supply.

Trains under construction; first new volumes ~2026-27.

The 2026-28 LNG supply wave (Qatar + US) likely cracks spot prices → coal-to-gas switching in Asia → demand response. Cycle, again.

Position for LNG glut mid/late-2020s, then the next under-investment shortage early 2030s. The cycle is the trade.

nodes.title: Sabine Pass / US Gulf LNG
nodes.body:
Cheniere's flagship; US = world #1 LNG exporter since 2023. FID→first cargo = 4-5 yrs.

Converted the US shale gas glut into geopolitical supply (replaced Russian pipe gas to Europe post-2022 — the fastest energy realignment ever executed).

Private firms + DOE export permits (the 2024 'pause' showed the political lever).

4-5 yrs per project; a wall of capacity arrives 2026-28.

US LNG = the marginal molecule setting both EU and Asia prices; Henry Hub↔TTF/JKM spreads = the arbitrage that funds it.

Energy flows rerouted in 18 months what diplomats argued over for 40 years. Markets > treaties when physics allows.

nodes.title: Crane CEC (ex-Three Mile Island 1)
nodes.body:
Constellation restarting TMI-1 (835 MW) under a 20-yr Microsoft PPA (Sep 2024) + $1B DOE loan. Target 2027.

The symbol-event: AI demand made a mothballed nuclear plant economic. But PJM analysis floated grid-connection slipping to 2031 (Constellation disputes) — the INTERCONNECTION QUEUE, not the reactor, is the fight.

Constellation + FERC/PJM rulings (decision window mid-2026).

Restart 2-4 yrs; the queue can add more than the restart takes.

If even a ready reactor with a hyperscaler PPA waits years for a grid slot, every datacenter timeline in PJM inherits that delay.

The most informative single project in US power: it isolates the bureaucratic constraint from the physical one.

nodes.title: Palisades — first US restart
nodes.body:
Holtec recommissioning (800 MW, closed 2022): NRC license restored Jul 2025, fuel loaded Oct 2025, return-to-service targeted 2026 — the first-ever US reactor restart. $1.52B DOE loan.

Proves the category: closed ≠ dead. Restart ≈ $1.5-2B and 2-4 yrs vs $35B and 10-15 yrs for new build (Vogtle).

Holtec + NRC + DOE financing.

Template now applies to a short list: Duane Arnold next.

Each success re-rates every retired-but-intact reactor as an option, and strengthens the 'restart > new-build' capital allocation.

Watch the gap between 'targeted 2026' and synchronized megawatts. Execution vs announcement is the whole game in nuclear.

Southern Company / Georgia ratepayers (who ate the overrun).

Next AP1000s would be cheaper (learning curve) — if anyone dares order one. Watch for exactly that.

nodes.title: GE Vernova — Greenville
nodes.body:
Heavy-duty gas turbine production hub. Order books (GEV, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi) effectively sold out to ~2028-29; buyers pay reservation fees for slots.

AI datacenters defaulted to gas for speed — and hit a manufacturing wall: turbine capacity was sized for a no-growth grid for 20 years.

Three OEMs globally. Oligopoly pricing power restored overnight.

Factory expansion: 2-3 yrs and OEMs are deliberately cautious (they remember 2000s overbuild).

Turbine slots now gate datacenter timelines as hard as GPUs do. Projects without 2026-27 slots slip to 2029+.

Picks-and-shovels logic, round two: the GPU of the power layer is a 9,000-ton machine from South Carolina.

nodes.title: Ashburn — Data Center Alley
nodes.body:
Densest datacenter cluster on Earth (Loudoun County, VA). US DC load: 4.4% of electricity (2023) → 6.7-12% by 2028 (LBNL, Dec 2024).

Fiber begat datacenters begat more fiber — 25 yrs of agglomeration. Now Dominion's interconnection queue and transformer scarcity bind the world's densest digital geography.

Dominion Energy's queue + Loudoun zoning politics (moratorium pressure rising).

New transmission into NoVA: late-2020s at best.

NoVA saturation pushes builds to TX/OH/GA/desert-Southwest — relocating the demand shock to new grids serially.

The bellwether grid. Whatever breaks here breaks everywhere else 2-3 years later.

nodes.body:
Flagship OpenAI/Oracle/SoftBank 'Stargate' campus (Jan 2025, $500B headline across sites). Onsite gas turbines to bypass the grid queue.

nodes.title: Three Gorges / China grid
nodes.body:
Symbol of scale: China generates ~2x US electricity, builds UHV transmission lines as routine, and added more solar in 2024-25 than the rest of the world combined.

In an AI race where power is the binding input, China's grid headroom + state-speed buildout is a structural advantage offsetting its chip disadvantage.

State Grid / NDRC — central planning's home turf.

—

If compute efficiency (DeepSeek-style) keeps reducing FLOPs-per-capability, the binding constraint shifts from chips (US edge) toward energy (CN edge). Watch this crossover.

The US-China AI race is chips vs electrons. Each side is strong where the other is constrained. The arbitrage closes via efficiency gains — track those hardest.

nodes.title: PJM Interconnection (queue)
nodes.body:
Largest US grid operator (65M people). National picture: ~2.6 TW of projects queued at end-2023, median wait ~5 yrs (LBNL 'Queued Up'); >95% of queue = solar/storage/wind.

The grid's bottleneck is paperwork + transmission studies, not generation technology. Electrons are slower than dollars because lawyers are slower than both.

FERC rules + regional operators + state permitting.

Queue reform underway; transmission lines still 8-12 yrs.

Every AI-power forecast that ignores queue physics overstates 2026-29 supply. Capacity prices in PJM already spiked 10x at the 2024-25 auction.

PJM capacity auctions = the bond market of the AI-power thesis. Price moves there validate or kill the whole narrative.

nodes.title: Egypt cable corridor
nodes.body:
~15-17 submarine cable systems funnel through Egypt (Red Sea→Med crossing); the majority of Europe↔Asia internet traffic.

Geography again: the same isthmus that makes Suez makes the internet's thinnest waist. Feb 2024: the drifting anchor of the Houthi-hit Rubymar cut 3 cables (Seacom, AAE-1, EIG).

Egypt (Telecom Egypt collects transit rent); cables share the threatened Red Sea with shipping.

New routes (overland via Saudi, around Africa) = years + Egypt actively defends its rent.

Multi-cable cut = Europe-Asia latency/capacity crisis; finance and cloud reroute via US (adding 100ms+ — real money in trading).

The internet has chokepoints just like oil. The repair-ship fleet (~60 vessels, old) is the hidden sub-constraint.

nodes.title: Singapore–Johor DC corridor
nodes.body:
Singapore: ~25 cable landings, SEA's hub — but a DC moratorium (2019-22) then strict caps pushed the build wave across the border: Johor became SEA's largest DC construction market.

Policy creates geography: one zoning decision relocated a billion-dollar industry 30km north into Malaysia.

Singapore (caps, green requirements) / Malaysia (courting the spillover, now hitting its own power/water limits).

Johor power constraints already emerging 2025-26.

The pattern repeats globally: DC demand flows to wherever power+land+permits clear fastest — a real-time map of regulatory arbitrage.

Trade the spillover geography, not the hub: the hub's scarcity is priced, the neighbor's windfall often isn't.

nodes.title: Luzon Strait cable choke
nodes.body:
The cable corridor east of Taiwan/north of Philippines. 2006 Hengchun earthquake cut ~8 cables — Asia's internet degraded for weeks. Matsu islands cables cut 2023 (Chinese vessels implicated).

Seismically active + militarily contested + carries trans-Pacific traffic: triple-risk overlay on one strait.

Nobody, which is the problem. Repair in contested water = the unsolved scenario.

Repair ships: weeks per cable in peacetime; unavailable in conflict.

Taiwan scenario = data severance alongside chip severance. Markets model the chips; few model the milliseconds.

Cable cuts are becoming a gray-zone language (Baltic 2023-24, Matsu, Red Sea). Learn to read it.

nodes.title: New York — dollar core
nodes.body:
USD: ~58% of FX reserves (IMF COFER), one side of ~88% of FX trades (BIS 2022), the invoicing default, and the Fed's swap lines = lender of last resort to the planet.

Network effects in money are the strongest that exist: everyone holds dollars because everyone holds dollars. Exorbitant privilege = the US borrows in what it prints.

Federal Reserve + OFAC (sanctions run through dollar clearing — control of the rail, not the territory).

De-dollarization is real but glacial: reserve share −10pts in ~25 yrs. Alternatives fragment (gold buying by CBs = the actual hedge flow).

Weaponization (2022 reserve freeze) accelerates the slow exit — every sanction is a marketing campaign for the alternatives.

The deepest moat and the slowest variable in this atlas. Decades, not years — but the direction is set and gold is the tell.

nodes.title: London — pricing & risk
nodes.body:
~38% of global FX turnover (BIS 2022); LME sets metal prices (and broke, nickel Mar 2022); Lloyd's prices war risk for the world's ships.

Empire's residue: the contracts, law (English law governs most trade docs), and insurance syndicates stayed after the ships left.

UK regulators; LME owned by HKEX (a quiet fact worth knowing since the 2022 nickel intervention favored a Chinese client).

—

Insurance withdrawal is the fastest blockade that exists: no underwriting = no sailing, no shots fired (the Taiwan and shadow-fleet mechanism).

Insurance pricing > think-tank reports. It's real money betting on real probabilities, updated daily.

nodes.title: SWIFT — La Hulpe
nodes.body:
The messaging layer of ~11,000 banks. 2022: Russian banks cut off — the canonical 'weaponized interdependence' strike.

Not money itself, just messages — but excommunication from the standard = friction tax on everything. Belgium hosts it; US/EU effectively command it.

G7 consensus in practice.

Alternatives exist (CIPS, SPFS, mBridge CBDC pilots) — small but compounding since 2022.

Each use of the weapon funds the alternatives. Same dynamic as the dollar node, faster clock.

Sanctions are a wasting asset: maximum power at first use, declining thereafter. Time-decay applies to weapons too.
